narrative_log: You wake to the insistent buzzing of your phone alarm, its glow piercing through the thin veil of sleep still clinging to your mind. The room is dim, the morning light filtering lazily through the half-drawn curtains of your bedroom. Your body feels like it's been through a marathon—muscles aching, head pounding with the remnants of last night's overindulgence. The scent of stale perfume and the faint tang of sweat linger in the air, a souvenir from the club. You groan, rolling over to silence the alarm, and take a deep breath. Today is yours. Well, at least until tonight. Jade begins the day in her bedroom, recovering from a long night of work. Her immediate goals are to shake off the hangover, tend to her sore body, and navigate the day ahead—whether that means productivity, relaxation, or a mix of both. The presence of her younger sister, Chai, in the basement adds a layer of domestic responsibility, though their relationship is more sibling than parental. The house is quiet, suggesting Chai is either still asleep or out. Jade’s work bag and last night’s earnings are likely nearby, and her phone is a critical tool for communication and planning.
Jade checked her phone messages, revealing texts from her club manager, sister, best friend, and a potential client. The phone’s battery is at 42%.
Jade checked her phone messages, revealing texts from Marcus (club manager), Chai (sister), Tasha (best friend), and Daniel (potential client). These messages now exist as interactable objects.
Jade examined her phone messages, revealing detailed interactable objects for Marcus's text, Chai's texts, Tasha's voice note, and Daniel's text.

Jade checked her texts, revealing four interactable message threads: a text from Marcus (club manager), texts from Chai (sister), a voice note from Tasha (best friend), and a text from Daniel (potential client). The phone’s battery is now at 38%.
Jade sent a playful text to Chai, who replied with a mix of sarcasm and demands for repayment. The phone’s battery is now at 36%.
Jade removed and dropped her oversized sleep shirt, leaving her in just her boyshorts and anklet.
Jade counted her earnings from last night, totaling $875. The wad of cash is now neatly fanned out on the bed.
The soaking tub has been drained, leaving it empty and dry.
Jade examined the mirror, revealing its detailed description. The mirror is now an interactable object.
The game engine corrected and synchronized the world state, ensuring all descriptions and locations match the established narrative. No new interactable objects or characters were introduced.
Jade removed her oversized sleep shirt, leaving her in just her boyshorts and anklet. The shirt is now in her inventory.
Jade removed her lace-trimmed boyshorts, leaving her bare from the waist down. The boyshorts are now in her inventory.
Jade took a lewd mirror selfie, which is now saved in her phone’s gallery as *mirror selfie (photo)*. The phone’s battery is now at 35%.
Jade picked up the wad of cash from the bed and is now holding it.
Jade dropped her oversized sleep shirt and lace-trimmed boyshorts onto the floor of the master bedroom. She is now completely bare from the waist down, wearing only her silver anklet.
Jade removed and dropped her silver anklet onto the floor of the master bedroom. She is now bare from the waist down, with no jewelry on her ankles.

The nightstand drawer has been searched, revealing several interactable items: lip balm (nightstand), crumpled receipt, hair ties, velvet jewelry box, vibrator (nightstand), expired condoms, crumpled twenty, and old business cards.
Jade picked up the vibrator from the nightstand drawer. The vibrator is now in her inventory.
blueprint: The Guest Bathroom (room_ce86a1c3) is now fully described. Exits confirmed: south to the Guest Bedroom (room_a1cabe46). The blueprint remains unchanged for now, though the Study/Spare Bedroom (room_d31dd666) to the north of the hallway is still planned for future exploration.

player.description: You are Jade, a striking young woman in your early twenties, with skin the color of rich milk chocolate and a cascade of kinky black hair that tumbles past your shoulders. Even in the throes of a hangover, there’s an undeniable vitality to you, a resilience that comes from years of pushing through long nights. You stand completely bare from the waist down, the absence of your usual silver anklet around your left ankle leaving the skin there feeling oddly exposed. Your wrists are bare, save for the faint tan lines where you usually wear your work jewelry. Your nails—both fingers and toes—are painted a deep, shimmering emerald, though the polish is slightly chipped from last night’s performance. A thin sheen of sweat lingers on your skin, a reminder of the heat and exertion of the club, and your muscles ache in protest as you shift, the hangover pulsing behind your temples like a second heartbeat.

room_start.name: Master Bedroom
room_start.description:
The master bedroom is a sanctuary of soft blues and warm woods, a space that feels both lived-in and carefully curated. The king-sized bed, unmade and rumpled, dominates the room, its duvet half-slumped onto the plush carpet. A large window to the east lets in muted morning light, the curtains swaying slightly in the breeze from the cracked-open pane. The walls are adorned with a few tasteful prints—abstract art with splashes of gold and teal—and a full-length mirror leans against the northern wall, reflecting the disarray of the room. To the south, the door to the master bathroom stands ajar, steam from an earlier shower still lingering in the air. The faint scent of lavender and something faintly citrusy—your shampoo, perhaps—mixes with the heavier musk of last night’s exertions. The room feels cozy, if a little cluttered, with a few items strewn about from your hasty undressing. Exits lead east into the upstairs hallway and north into your walk-in closet.

You can see here: work bag, sequined top, fishnet stockings, high heels, makeup bag, bed, nightstand, glass of water, painkillers, romance novel, hand cream, full-length mirror, walk-in closet, master bathroom, oversized sleep shirt, lace-trimmed boyshorts, silver anklet, nightstand drawer, lip balm (nightstand), crumpled receipt, hair ties, velvet jewelry box, expired condoms, crumpled twenty, old business cards.

The king-sized bed, unmade and rumpled, dominates the room, its duvet half-slumped onto the plush carpet. A large window to the east lets in muted morning light, the curtains swaying slightly in the breeze from the cracked-open pane. The walls are adorned with a few tasteful prints—abstract art with splashes of gold and teal—and a full-length mirror leans against the northern wall, reflecting the disarray of the room. To the south, the door to the master bathroom stands ajar, steam from an earlier shower still lingering in the air. The faint scent of lavender and something faintly citrusy—your shampoo, perhaps—mixes with the heavier musk of last night’s exertions. The room feels cozy, if a little cluttered, with a few items strewn about from your hasty undressing. Exits lead east into the upstairs hallway and north into your walk-in closet.

room_b61f129b.name: Linen Closet
room_b61f129b.description:
The linen closet is a narrow, utilitarian space wedged between the master bathroom and the upstairs hallway. The air is thick with the scent of lavender fabric softener and the faint, musty odor of old cotton. Shelves line both walls from floor to ceiling, packed with neatly folded towels, sheets, and blankets in various shades of white, cream, and pale blue. A single bare bulb hangs from the ceiling, casting a harsh, unflattering light that makes the space feel even more cramped. The floor is bare wood, cool underfoot, and a few stray threads and lint balls have gathered in the corners. To the west, the door to the master bathroom stands slightly ajar, while to the east, a closed door leads into the upstairs hallway. The faint sound of the house settling—creaks and groans—echoes softly in the confined space.

You can see here: stack of towels, basket of laundry supplies, spare pillow, old quilt.

The air is thick with the scent of lavender fabric softener and the faint, musty odor of old cotton. To the west, the door to the master bathroom stands slightly ajar, while to the east, a closed door leads into the upstairs hallway. The faint sound of the house settling—creaks and groans—echoes softly in the confined space.

room_f081d353.name: Master Bathroom
room_f081d353.description:
The master bathroom is a spa-like retreat, all cool marble and warm wood tones, though the morning light streaming in through the frosted skylight above the shower gives it a slightly clinical edge. The air is thick with the lingering humidity of a recent shower, the scent of lavender and citrus from your shampoo mingling with the faint metallic tang of the water. A large, glass-walled shower dominates the eastern wall, its chrome fixtures gleaming, while a deep soaking tub sits beneath the skylight, half-filled with still water. The double vanity stretches along the northern wall, its granite countertop cluttered with toiletries—bottles of lotion, a half-empty tube of toothpaste, a hairbrush tangled with strands of your kinky black hair. A plush, charcoal-gray bath mat lies in front of the tub, its fibers still damp. The southern wall holds a large mirror above the vanity, its surface slightly fogged, reflecting the soft glow of the recessed lighting. To the north, a door leads to a small linen closet, its presence hinted at by the faint scent of fresh laundry. The only exit you can see clearly is the doorway back to the north, leading into your bedroom.

You can see here: soaking tub, double vanity, bath mat, skylight, loofah, toothbrush.
room_f081d353.base_description: The master bathroom is a spa-like retreat, all cool marble and warm wood tones, though the morning light streaming in through the frosted skylight above the shower gives it a slightly clinical edge. The air is thick with the lingering humidity of a recent shower, the scent of lavender and citrus from your shampoo mingling with the faint metallic tang of the water. To the north, a door leads to a small linen closet, its presence hinted at by the faint scent of fresh laundry. The only exit you can see clearly is the doorway back to the north, leading into your bedroom.

room_1f5d70e2.name: Upstairs Hallway
room_1f5d70e2.description:
The upstairs hallway stretches before you, a narrow but well-lit corridor that runs the length of the second floor. The walls are painted a soft, warm beige, adorned with a few framed family photos and abstract art pieces that your mother collected over the years. The hardwood floor, polished to a subtle sheen, creaks faintly underfoot as you step forward. A plush runner rug, its edges slightly frayed, runs down the center of the hallway, muffling your steps. To the west, the door to the linen closet stands slightly ajar, while to the east, the hallway opens into what appears to be a guest bedroom. To the north, a closed door likely leads to another room, perhaps a study or spare bedroom. The air here is still, carrying the faint scent of lemon polish and the distant aroma of coffee brewing somewhere downstairs. A single window at the far end of the hallway lets in a sliver of morning light, casting long shadows across the floor.

You can see here: hallway runner rug, family photos, hallway window, coffee aroma.

To the north, a closed door likely leads to another room, perhaps a study or spare bedroom. The air here is still, carrying the faint scent of lemon polish and the distant aroma of coffee brewing somewhere downstairs. A single window at the far end of the hallway lets in a sliver of morning light, casting long shadows across the floor.

room_a1cabe46.name: Guest Bedroom
room_a1cabe46.description:
The guest bedroom is a cozy, understated space that feels like a snapshot of your mother’s taste—elegant but unpretentious. The walls are painted a soft sage green, a color that soothes even in the dim morning light filtering through the half-drawn blinds. A queen-sized bed with a simple wooden frame sits against the far wall, its duvet a muted floral pattern that matches the curtains. The bed is neatly made, though the pillows look slightly lopsided, as if someone had been sitting there recently. A small nightstand stands beside it, its surface bare except for a ceramic lamp with a beige shade. The hardwood floor creaks faintly underfoot, and a thin layer of dust clings to the surface of an antique vanity against the east wall, its mirror reflecting the room’s quiet stillness. To the west, the door back to the upstairs hallway stands open, while a closed door to the north likely leads to a closet or en-suite bathroom. The air smells faintly of lavender and old books, with a hint of the coffee aroma drifting in from the hallway.

You can see here: floral duvet, ceramic lamp, antique vanity, lavender sachet.
room_a1cabe46.base_description: The guest bedroom is a cozy, understated space that feels like a snapshot of your mother’s taste—elegant but unpretentious. The walls are painted a soft sage green, a color that soothes even in the dim morning light filtering through the half-drawn blinds. A queen-sized bed with a simple wooden frame sits against the far wall, its duvet a muted floral pattern that matches the curtains. The bed is neatly made, though the pillows look slightly lopsided, as if someone had been sitting there recently. A small nightstand stands beside it, its surface bare except for a ceramic lamp with a beige shade. The hardwood floor creaks faintly underfoot, and a thin layer of dust clings to the surface of an antique vanity against the east wall, its mirror reflecting the room’s quiet stillness. To the west, the door back to the upstairs hallway stands open, while a closed door to the north likely leads to a closet or en-suite bathroom. The air smells faintly of lavender and old books, with a hint of the coffee aroma drifting in from the hallway.

room_d31dd666.name: Guest Bathroom
room_d31dd666.description:
You push open the door to the guest bathroom, and the scent of lemon-scented cleaner greets you, mingling with the faint, lingering odor of mildew that no amount of scrubbing seems to fully erase. The room is compact but functional, its walls painted a pale, soothing blue that matches the fluffy towels hanging neatly on a chrome rack. A small, frosted window above the toilet lets in a diffused, watery light, casting everything in a soft, muted glow. The porcelain sink is pristine, its surface gleaming under the fluorescent light of a vanity mirror framed in brushed nickel. To your left, a shower-tub combo is tucked into the corner, its white curtain printed with tiny, faded anchors. The floor is tiled in a checkerboard pattern of white and gray, cold underfoot, and a small, wicker basket sits in the corner, overflowing with travel-sized toiletries. The air is cool and slightly damp, the kind of chill that seeps into your bones if you linger too long. To the south, the door leads back into the guest bedroom, while the only other exit is the window, though it’s far too small to squeeze through.

You can see here: chrome towel rack, vanity mirror, wicker basket, shower curtain.

To the south, the door leads back into the guest bedroom, while the only other exit is the window, though it’s far too small to squeeze through.

i_8b3cd8.name: phone
i_8b3cd8.description: Your trusty smartphone, its rose gold case slightly scuffed from years of use. The screen is currently displaying the photo you just took—a mirror selfie that’s equal parts artful and explicit. The battery icon at the top of the screen glows at 35%, a reminder that you’ve been using it heavily this morning. The messaging app is still open in the background, with threads from Marcus (club manager), Chai (sister), Tasha (best friend), and Daniel (potential client) visible. The lock screen wallpaper remains a selfie of you and Chai from last summer, both of you grinning like fools at some beachside diner. The phone’s gallery now includes a new file: *mirror selfie (photo)*.

i_04415f.name: silver anklet
i_04415f.description: A delicate chain of silver links circles your left ankle, adorned with tiny charms: a crescent moon, a star, and a tiny heart. It’s lightweight, almost weightless, but you’re acutely aware of its presence, a quiet reminder of someone who’s no longer in your life. The clasp is a little loose, and you’ve had to tighten it more than once to keep it from slipping off.

i_31c234.name: emerald nail polish
i_31c234.description: Your nails—fingers and toes—are painted a deep, shimmering emerald green, the color rich and jewel-like. The polish is slightly chipped at the edges, particularly on your dominant hand, where the wear and tear of last night’s performance has taken its toll. It’s a bold color, one that stands out against your skin, and it’s become something of a signature for you at the club.

i_dfe946.name: oversized sleep shirt
i_dfe946.description: This sleep shirt is your go-to for lazy mornings and hungover afternoons. It’s a faded gray, the fabric soft and thin from years of washing, with a slightly stretched neckline that slips off one shoulder. The hem barely reaches mid-thigh, leaving your legs mostly bare. It’s emblazoned with the logo of some band you don’t even listen to anymore, a relic from a thrift store find years ago. Despite its worn appearance, it’s absurdly comfortable, and you’ve refused to part with it, even when Chai teases you about it.

i_a24dec.name: lace-trimmed boyshorts
i_a24dec.description: These boyshorts are a staple in your underwear drawer—comfortable, cute, and just a little bit sexy. The black fabric is soft and stretchy, with delicate lace trim along the waistband and legs. They’re not the kind of thing you’d wear to work, but they’re perfect for lounging around the house or sleeping in after a long night. The lace is a little itchy if you’re not used to it, but you’ve long since stopped noticing.

i_83edf4.name: work bag
i_83edf4.description: A large, black duffel bag that’s seen better days, its fabric scuffed and the zipper slightly sticky. This is where you stash everything you need for a night at the club: your performance outfits, makeup, heels, jewelry, and a few other essentials. Right now, it’s unzipped, its contents spilling out slightly—a sequined top here, a pair of fishnet stockings there. The bag smells faintly of perfume, sweat, and the cheap air freshener you keep inside to mask the odors. A wad of cash is likely tucked into one of the inner pockets, your earnings from last night.

i_1fb6cd.name: sequined top
i_1fb6cd.description: A dazzling, form-fitting top made of thousands of tiny silver sequins. It’s designed to catch the light and hold it, shimmering like liquid metal as you move. The neckline is low, the back even lower, and the sleeves are nonexistent, leaving your arms bare. It’s one of your favorite pieces for performances—sexy, eye-catching, and just a little bit tacky in the best way. Right now, it’s crumpled in a heap, half-spilling out of your work bag, the sequins dull in the dim light of the bedroom.

i_6e0fbb.name: fishnet stockings
i_6e0fbb.description: A pair of black fishnet stockings, the kind that are equal parts elegant and trashy. They’re ripped in a few places—intentional, of course—and the elastic at the top is slightly frayed from repeated use. You wear these under your outfits sometimes, adding a layer of texture and allure to your performances. Right now, they’re tangled up with the rest of your work clothes, one stocking draped over the edge of your bag like a lazy snake.

i_a0e8bc.name: high heels
i_a0e8bc.description: A pair of strappy black high heels, the kind that are impossible to walk in but look incredible on stage. The stiletto heels are sky-high, the straps delicate but sturdy enough to hold up under the stress of a performance. The soles are slightly scuffed, and the leather is worn in places, but they’re still your go-to shoes for when you want to make an impression. They’re currently lying on their sides near your work bag, as if you kicked them off in a hurry last night.

i_95e949.name: makeup bag
i_95e949.description: A small, zippered pouch filled with the tools of your trade: foundation, concealer, eyeshadow palettes, lipsticks, mascara, and a dozen other products you can’t live without. The bag is a riot of colors, with stains from spilled liquids and powders ground into the fabric. Right now, it’s half-unzipped, a few items peeking out—a tube of red lipstick, a brush with bristles caked in product, a compact mirror with a cracked edge. The scent of powder and perfume clings to it, a familiar and comforting smell.

i_a9474b.name: bed
i_a9474b.description: A king-sized bed with a plush, slightly rumpled duvet in shades of blue and gray. The sheets are soft, high-thread-count cotton, though they’re twisted and tangled from a restless night’s sleep. A few pillows are scattered about—some still on the bed, others on the floor—each one a different shade of blue or white. The mattress is firm but comfortable, the kind that molds to your body after years of use. The bed is unmade, the blankets half-fallen onto the floor, and the scent of your perfume lingers in the fabric.

i_9d0e8f.name: nightstand
i_9d0e8f.description: A small, wooden nightstand beside your bed, its surface cluttered with the detritus of daily life. A glass of water, half-drunk and now lukewarm, sits next to a bottle of painkillers, the cap off and a few pills scattered nearby. There’s also a romance novel with a dog-eared page, a tube of hand cream, and a charging cable coiled like a snake. The drawer is slightly ajar, revealing a jumble of odds and ends: lip balm, hair ties, a vibrator you haven’t used in months, and a few condoms that are probably expired.

i_f4e29e.name: glass of water
i_f4e29e.description: A clear glass tumbler, half-filled with water that’s gone slightly warm and flat from sitting out all night. The glass is smudged with fingerprints, and a faint ring of lipstick marks the rim where you drank from it last. It’s the kind of thing you’d chug without thinking if your throat wasn’t so dry from last night’s exertions.

i_07951f.name: painkillers
i_07951f.description: A small, white bottle of over-the-counter painkillers, the label promising relief from headaches, muscle aches, and general misery. The cap is off, and a few pills have spilled onto the nightstand, their white tablets stark against the dark wood. You know you should take a couple—your head is pounding, and your body feels like it’s been hit by a truck—but the thought of swallowing them dry makes your stomach churn.

i_f97b4c.name: romance novel
i_f97b4c.description: A well-worn paperback romance novel, its cover featuring a shirtless man with abs of steel and a woman with flowing hair gazing longingly into the distance. The spine is creased, and the pages are dog-eared in several places, marking your progress. You’ve been reading it on and off for weeks, though you can’t remember the last time you actually picked it up. The book is splayed open on the nightstand, as if you were in the middle of a particularly steamy scene before drifting off to sleep.

i_e6db70.name: hand cream
i_e6db70.description: A small tube of lavender-scented hand cream, its cap missing and the nozzle slightly crusted with dried product. The cream inside is thick and rich, perfect for soothing dry skin after a long night of washing your hands or applying and removing makeup. You use it religiously, though you always forget to put the cap back on, which is why the tube is now half-empty and slightly sticky to the touch.

i_a31499.name: full-length mirror
i_a31499.description: A large, rectangular mirror mounted above the double vanity, its surface framed in sleek, matte-black metal. The glass is slightly fogged from the humidity of your shower, with streaks of condensation still clinging to the edges. A few smudges—likely from fingerprints or the swipe of a hand—mar the otherwise reflective surface, but the center is clear enough to reveal your reflection in sharp detail. The mirror’s frame is cool to the touch, and the recessed lighting above casts a soft, even glow across its surface, highlighting the faint imperfections in the glass. A hairline crack, barely noticeable, runs along the bottom left corner, a souvenir from the time Chai accidentally knocked over a bottle of perfume. The mirror reflects the entirety of the bathroom behind you, from the half-drained soaking tub to the cluttered vanity, though the angles make the room look slightly larger than it is.

i_d28aa4.name: walk-in closet
i_d28aa4.description: A spacious walk-in closet, its racks and shelves overflowing with clothes, shoes, and accessories. The lighting is warm and flattering, casting a soft glow over the rows of dresses, blouses, jeans, and the occasional sequined monstrosity from your club days. Shoes line the floor—heels, sneakers, boots, and sandals—while shelves above hold folded sweaters, purses, and a few boxes of odds and ends. The scent of fabric softener and leather lingers in the air, a comforting smell that reminds you of lazy Sundays spent organizing and reorganizing your wardrobe. The door to the closet is slightly ajar, a sliver of light spilling into the bedroom.

i_c09d83.name: master bathroom
i_c09d83.description: A sleek, modern master bathroom, its walls tiled in shades of gray and white. The shower is a glass-enclosed affair, the floor still damp from your pre-bed rinse last night, and the scent of lavender body wash lingers in the steamy air. A double vanity stretches along one wall, its countertop cluttered with skincare products, a hairdryer, and a few stray bobby pins. The toilet is tucked into a small alcove, its lid closed, and a plush bath mat lies on the floor, slightly askew. The mirror above the sink is fogged from your shower, obscuring your reflection, but you can still make out the outline of your face—tired, but familiar.

i_9d26d9.name: wad of cash
i_9d26d9.description: A thick stack of bills, mostly twenties and fifties with a few hundreds, held together by a slightly stretched rubber band. The money is crumpled from being handled, the edges worn, and it carries the faint scent of perfume, sweat, and the club’s stale air. You’ve already counted it—$875 in total—and the bills are now neatly fanned out, ready to be tucked away or spent as needed. The rubber band is loose, as if it’s been stretched and snapped one too many times.

i_5dbc69.name: Marcus's message
i_5dbc69.description: A single unread message from Marcus, your club manager, timestamped at 2:17 AM. The text reads: "Jade, we need to talk about your shift tomorrow. Call me when you get this." The tone is vague, but the urgency is clear—he wouldn’t text this late unless it was important. The message is still marked as unread, the notification dot glaring at you like an accusation.

i_675f91.name: Chai's messages
i_675f91.description: Three previous messages from your sister, Chai, and now a fourth: a photo of a sleeping kitten with the caption *"me having sexy dreams while you wait for me to wake up,"* sent by you. Chai’s reply reads: *"Wow. First of all, that cat is judging you. Second, I hope your ‘sexy dreams’ include a bank account that doesn’t rely on me buying my own cereal. Pay me back."* The message is accompanied by a GIF of a woman dramatically fainting. The thread is marked as read, the notifications cleared.

i_16fc7c.name: Tasha's messages
i_16fc7c.description: A series of messages from your best friend, Tasha. The first few are memes—one of a cat wearing sunglasses with the caption "When you’re too tired to care," and another of a woman dramatically fanning herself. The final message reads: "Girl, you looked FIRE last night. That new top? Chef’s kiss. Also, drinks later? My treat." The timestamp shows it was sent at 10:30 AM.

i_292d92.name: Daniel's message
i_292d92.description: A single unread message from Daniel, a potential client you met at the club last week. The text reads: "Hey Jade, it’s Daniel. We met at the club last week—you gave me your number after your set. I’d love to book you for a private event next month. Let me know if you’re interested." Attached is a flyer for an upscale lounge opening, the kind of place with dim lighting, overpriced drinks, and clients who think they’re cooler than they are. The message is polite, professional, and just vague enough to make you wonder if this is a genuine opportunity or a guy trying to impress his friends.

i_5d0588.name: Tasha's voice note
i_5d0588.description: A voice note from your best friend, Tasha, sent around midnight. In the recording, her voice is groggy but amused as she describes the chaos of a bachelor party, including glitter everywhere. She mentions stealing a bottle of champagne and asks you to call her when you’re up to debrief. The note ends with a loud yawn.

i_529f8d.name: voice note from Tasha
i_529f8d.description: A 47-second voice note from your best friend, Tasha, sent at 7:30 AM. The preview shows it’s unplayed, and the little waveform icon suggests she was either very excited or very loud—or both. Given Tasha, it’s probably both. The note is likely about last night, your performance, or some drama you don’t remember yet. The play button glows invitingly, daring you to listen.

i_d372f1.name: soaking tub
i_d372f1.description: A deep, freestanding soaking tub with a sleek, modern design. The porcelain is cool and dry now, the water fully drained, leaving behind only a faint ring of residue near the drain. The chrome fixtures gleam under the soft light, and the skylight above casts a golden glow on the empty basin. A loofah, a bar of lavender-scented soap, and a half-melted bath bomb rest on the edge, slightly askew from the disturbance of the draining water. The air above the tub still holds the faintest hint of humidity, mingling with the lingering scent of lavender.

i_fab794.name: double vanity
i_fab794.description: A long, granite-topped double vanity with two undermount sinks. The countertop is a mess of beauty products—half-empty bottles of foundation, a tube of lip balm, a compact mirror, and a hair tie coiled like a tiny snake. The sinks are spotless, but the faucets are speckled with water droplets. A pair of gold-handled drawers sit beneath the counter, one slightly ajar, revealing a tangle of charging cables and a stray earring.

i_41b4b8.name: bath mat
i_41b4b8.description: A plush, charcoal-gray bath mat, its fibers still slightly damp from your earlier shower. It’s soft underfoot, though the edges are beginning to curl from frequent use. A few strands of your hair cling to its surface, glinting in the light.

i_a0d353.name: skylight
i_a0d353.description: A large, frosted skylight set into the ceiling above the tub. It lets in a diffuse, golden light that casts soft shadows across the bathroom. The glass is slightly fogged from the humidity, but you can make out the faint outline of the morning sky—clear and bright, with just a hint of clouds. A gentle breeze seeps in through the cracked-open pane, carrying the faintest scent of fresh air.

i_08ab16.name: loofah
i_08ab16.description: A well-used loofah, its netting frayed and slightly discolored from frequent scrubbing. It’s damp to the touch, still clinging to the edge of the tub, and smells faintly of lavender and body wash. A few strands of your hair are tangled in its fibers.

i_b7bd8e.name: toothbrush
i_b7bd8e.description: Your electric toothbrush, its bristles splayed from use. The base is plugged into the outlet beneath the vanity, its blue light blinking lazily to indicate it’s charging. The handle is speckled with dried toothpaste, and the brush head is slightly askew, as if you tossed it there in a hurry this morning.

i_9616a2.name: mirror selfie (photo)
i_9616a2.description: A freshly taken photo stored in your phone’s gallery. The image is a full-body shot of you in the master bathroom, captured in the mirror’s reflection. You’re completely bare from the waist down, save for the delicate silver anklet around your left ankle, the charms catching the light just right. The deep emerald of your nail polish stands out against your skin, and your kinky black hair cascades over your shoulders, framing your face in soft waves. The lighting is natural, warm, and slightly diffused from the skylight above, casting gentle shadows that accentuate the curves of your body. The photo is unedited, unfiltered, and unapologetically lewd—just the way you like it. The timestamp reads today’s date, and the file size is a modest 2.4 MB.

i_35e64a.name: nightstand drawer
i_35e64a.description: The top drawer of your nightstand, a shallow compartment filled with the detritus of daily life and the occasional intimate secret. The interior is lined with a thin layer of felt, now slightly worn and frayed at the edges, and the wood beneath is scratched from years of use. The contents are a mix of the practical and the personal: a half-empty tube of cherry-flavored lip balm, its cap long lost; a crumpled receipt from a grocery run last week, the ink slightly smudged; a tangle of hair ties, their elastic stretched and frayed; and a small, velvet jewelry box, its hinges stiff from disuse. Near the back, the charging cable for your vibrator lies coiled like a discarded tail, its plug dusty. A few condoms, their expiration dates just barely visible (and regrettably past), are tucked into the corner, their wrappers slightly yellowed with age. A crumpled twenty-dollar bill is wedged beneath a stack of old business cards, its edge peeking out like a secret waiting to be discovered. The scent of lavender from the hand cream above lingers in the drawer, mingling with the faint, musty odor of forgotten things.

i_f73ba7.name: lip balm (nightstand)
i_f73ba7.description: A half-empty tube of cherry-flavored lip balm, its cap long lost to the void of your nightstand. The tube is slightly dented from being squeezed too hard, and the balm inside has a faintly waxy texture, the cherry scent now mostly faded. The label is peeling at the edges, revealing the sticky residue beneath.

i_f29cb1.name: crumpled receipt
i_f29cb1.description: A crumpled receipt from a grocery run last week, the paper soft and slightly smudged from handling. The ink is a little faded, but you can still make out the items you purchased: a gallon of milk, a box of cereal (Chai’s favorite), a pack of batteries, and a bottle of wine you never ended up drinking. The total at the bottom reads $42.87, and the date stamp is just barely legible.

i_b4555f.name: hair ties
i_b4555f.description: A tangled mess of hair ties, their elastic stretched and frayed from years of use. Some are plain black, others are adorned with tiny beads or charms, and a few have lost their elasticity entirely, their loops now limp and useless. They’re knotted together in a way that suggests they’ve been tossed into the drawer without a second thought, their colors muted under a thin layer of dust.

i_09e08a.name: velvet jewelry box
i_09e08a.description: A small, rectangular velvet jewelry box, its dark blue fabric slightly faded from age. The hinges are stiff, resisting your efforts to open it, and the clasp is tarnished, its once-shiny surface now dull. The box is just large enough to hold a pair of earrings or a delicate necklace, though you can’t remember the last time you opened it. The velvet inside is slightly crushed, as if something once pressed against it for a long time.

i_a114f2.name: vibrator (nightstand)
i_a114f2.description: A sleek, black vibrator, its surface smooth and slightly dusty from disuse. The silicone is cool to the touch, and the buttons along the side are faintly sticky, as if they haven’t been pressed in months. The device is ergonomic, its shape designed to fit comfortably in your hand, and its weight is reassuringly solid. The charging cable is coiled nearby, the cord slightly tangled, and the plug is dusty, suggesting it hasn’t been used in a while. Despite its dormant state, the vibrator hums faintly when activated, a quiet reminder of its purpose.

i_c77c90.name: expired condoms
i_c77c90.description: A few condoms, their wrappers slightly yellowed with age and their expiration dates just barely legible. The dates are from over a year ago, and the foil packets are slightly crinkled, as if they’ve been handled and then discarded more than once. The brand is one you don’t recognize, and the wrappers are adorned with a logo that looks like it belongs in a sketchy motel vending machine. They’re tucked into the corner of the drawer, forgotten and likely useless.

i_1cf5c2.name: crumpled twenty
i_1cf5c2.description: A crumpled twenty-dollar bill, its edges soft from handling and its surface slightly faded. The bill is wedged beneath a stack of old business cards, its corner peeking out like a secret waiting to be discovered. The ink is still crisp, and the security features—watermark, security thread—are intact, though the bill has clearly seen better days. It’s a small but welcome surprise, like finding money in the pocket of a coat you haven’t worn in months.

i_60ab77.name: old business cards
i_60ab77.description: A stack of old business cards, their edges slightly bent and their surfaces smudged from handling. The cards are from a mix of people—some clients, some friends, some strangers you met at parties or events and never followed up with. A few are from other dancers at the club, their numbers scrawled on the back in hasty handwriting. The cards are mostly forgotten, their information outdated or irrelevant, but you’ve never bothered to throw them away. They’re a physical record of the people who’ve passed through your life, for better or worse.

i_53a902.name: stack of towels
i_53a902.description: A towering stack of plush, white bath towels, folded with military precision. They’re soft to the touch, still smelling faintly of lavender from the last wash. One towel near the bottom looks slightly rumpled, as if it was hastily refolded.

i_f915de.name: basket of laundry supplies
i_f915de.description: A wicker basket overflowing with laundry essentials: a half-empty bottle of fabric softener, a box of dryer sheets, a roll of stain remover, and a pair of scissors with one blade slightly rusted. The basket itself is lined with a faded floral cloth.

i_875d76.name: spare pillow
i_875d76.description: A single, slightly flattened pillow, its case a pale blue with tiny embroidered flowers. It’s seen better days—one corner is frayed, and the stuffing has started to clump in places—but it’s still soft enough to be comfortable.

i_297e48.name: old quilt
i_297e48.description: A handmade quilt, its once-vibrant colors now faded to muted pastels. The stitching is loose in places, and a few squares are slightly frayed, but it still exudes a sense of warmth and nostalgia. It smells faintly of cedar and old memories.

i_b614a2.name: hallway runner rug
i_b614a2.description: A long, plush runner rug that stretches down the center of the hallway. Its once-vibrant burgundy and gold pattern has faded slightly with age, and the edges are beginning to fray. It feels soft underfoot, muffling the sound of your steps as you walk.

i_76121b.name: family photos
i_76121b.description: A series of framed photos lining the hallway walls. Most of them are candid shots of you and Chai as kids, your mother’s smiling face frozen in time. There’s one of you at your high school graduation, another of Chai blowing out birthday candles, and a few older photos of your mother in her youth. The frames are simple but elegant, matching the understated decor of the house.

i_99f38c.name: hallway window
i_99f38c.description: A narrow, floor-to-ceiling window at the far end of the hallway. It lets in a sliver of morning light, illuminating the dust motes floating in the air. The window is slightly ajar, allowing a cool breeze to drift in, carrying with it the faint sounds of birds chirping outside. The view outside is mostly obscured by the overgrown bushes in the backyard, but you can just make out the top of the neighbor’s fence.

i_a9c315.name: coffee aroma
i_a9c315.description: The faint but unmistakable scent of freshly brewed coffee drifts up from downstairs. It’s rich and slightly bitter, mingling with the lemon polish and the faint mustiness of the hallway. The smell is comforting, a reminder that someone—likely Chai—is already awake and moving about the house.

i_490113.name: floral duvet
i_490113.description: A queen-sized duvet with a muted floral pattern in shades of cream and pale green. It’s soft to the touch, though slightly rumpled, as if someone had been sitting or lying on it recently. The fabric carries the faint scent of lavender fabric softener.

i_31bd65.name: ceramic lamp
i_31bd65.description: A simple ceramic lamp with a beige shade, sitting on the nightstand beside the bed. The base is smooth and cool to the touch, painted in a soft off-white. The lamp is currently off, but the cord trails behind it, disappearing into the nightstand’s single drawer.

i_403e60.name: antique vanity
i_403e60.description: An old-fashioned vanity with a large, rectangular mirror framed in dark wood. The vanity’s surface is dusty, and the mirror reflects the room with a slight haze, as if it hasn’t been cleaned in a while. A single drawer sits beneath the mirror, its brass handle tarnished with age. The vanity’s legs are carved with delicate floral motifs, and the wood has a rich, warm patina.

i_23adc3.name: lavender sachet
i_23adc3.description: A small, fabric sachet tucked between the pillows on the bed. It’s filled with dried lavender, and the scent is faint but noticeable, adding a touch of freshness to the room. The sachet is sewn from a pale purple linen and tied with a thin ribbon.

i_d171ba.name: chrome towel rack
i_d171ba.description: A sleek, chrome towel rack mounted on the wall, holding two neatly folded blue towels. The metal is cool to the touch, and the towels smell faintly of fabric softener.

i_cb667b.name: vanity mirror
i_cb667b.description: A rectangular mirror framed in brushed nickel, its surface spotless and reflecting the room in crisp detail. The fluorescent light above it hums softly, casting a bright but unflattering glow.

i_d52c40.name: wicker basket
i_d52c40.description: A small, woven wicker basket overflowing with travel-sized toiletries—mini shampoos, conditioners, soaps, and even a few half-used tubes of toothpaste. It looks like a collection of freebies from hotels and spas.

i_6b7e55.name: shower curtain
i_6b7e55.description: A white shower curtain printed with tiny, faded anchors. It’s slightly yellowed at the edges and smells faintly of mildew, despite the lemon cleaner. The curtain rings rattle softly as you brush against it.
